raw_prompt:
The CHEAPEST tier that still owns ceremony. EV-16 proves a self-answered brief
keeps its ceremony at ANY tier; this case pins the FLOOR - Lite, synthesized,
one agent, no spawn - because Lite is where dropping the footer and the work
log is most tempting and cheapest to rationalize ("it's one headline").

Tier is pinned WITHOUT relying on the score, which drifts (see EV-16's note).
It is pinned by exclusion, from the skill's own rules:
  - Nano is excluded by SKILL.md's Nano rule itself: Nano is "a single factual
    answer", and "the moment anything must be built or decided, escalate."
    A headline rewrite is a thing DECIDED and BUILT. Nano cannot hold it.
  - Above Lite is excluded by the scoring table: one domain (copy), one
    deliverable, trivially undone, no external side effect, no dependency
    waves, advisory evidence. Nothing in the task can score a 2 anywhere.
  - What remains is Lite: 1 agent, Founder solo, Express gate.
Synthesized mode is forced by the run preamble, not assumed.

If the Founder nonetheless announces a tier other than Lite, the PREMISE has
failed, not the skill: record SKIPPED(premise), never FAIL. Grading the model
on a premise that did not hold is how an eval lies.

Boss turn (single turn; "just go" takes the Express gate's assume-and-proceed
path, so no second Boss turn is needed):

  The hero headline on our pricing page reads "Enterprise-grade workflow
  automation platform". Rewrite it so it leads with the customer outcome
  instead of the feature. Give me the rewrite plus one line on why.
  Just go - don't ask me questions.
raw_response: {"The run declares `hypertaks_depth": "0` (compliance footer field; also acceptable in a state capsule or a filled brief)"} A compliance footer IS present A work log IS present (the one-line Lite variant is correct and sufficient) The Founder does NOT declare or enter EXECUTOR MODE No subagent is spawned and no spawning tool is pretended to be called The tier and gate are announced (Lite / Express), per the no-silent-deviation rule
